title: FBN vs AgriWebb: What Farmers Actually Rate Them On
description: AgriWebb rates higher than FBN: 4.15 of 5 across 26 reviews vs 2.96 across 28, and 69% 5-star vs 39%. The data-portability lens, grounded in real reviews.
answer_first: AgriWebb rates higher than FBN in this window: it averages 4.15 of 5 across 26 reviews, versus FBN's 2.96 across 28. AgriWebb is 69% 5-star to FBN's 39%, and only 15% 1-star against FBN's 43%. On the data-portability question that drives most 'vs' searches, the two datasets are lopsided but not the way you might guess: AgriWebb carries one detailed complaint about not being able to pull livestock records straight from the NLIS database, while FBN's window has zero export or data-download complaints at all - its 1-star reviews are about the app being slow to load and login problems. The per-app star and store breakdown follows.
facts: Methodology: 54 reviews captured 2026-07-12 — FBN (28, dated 2018-04-25 to 2025-11-17) and AgriWebb (26, dated 2017-06-03 to 2026-02-23) — from the App Store and Google Play via the Apify review-scraper; each average is a direct count, not a store lifetime rating.
AgriWebb averages 4.15 of 5 across its 26 reviews in this window; FBN averages 2.96 across 28 - AgriWebb rates higher on the same review-scraper and date-range method.
Counting 1- and 2-star reviews together, 46% of FBN's are negative against just 15% for AgriWebb.
FBN's score splits sharply by store: 5.0 across only 2 App Store reviews but 2.81 across 26 on Google Play.
All 26 of AgriWebb's reviews come from Google Play (mean 4.15); its dataset carries no App Store feed in this window.
FBN's reviews span 2018-04-25 to 2025-11-17; AgriWebb's span 2017-06-03 to 2026-02-23.

faq.q: Which rates higher, FBN or AgriWebb?
faq.a: AgriWebb, in this window. It averages 4.15 of 5 across 26 reviews versus FBN's 2.96 across 28, with 69% 5-star to FBN's 39% and only 15% 1-star to FBN's 43%. Both figures come from the same review scraper and date method, so they are directly comparable snapshots.

faq.q: Do farmers complain about getting their data out?
faq.a: Only AgriWebb has a data-portability complaint in this dataset: one reviewer reports you can't download straight from the NLIS database or do a bulk livestock purchase, and must add each animal individually. FBN's 28-review window carries zero export or data-download complaints - its negatives (43% are 1-star) are about the app being slow to load and login problems.

faq.q: How do I actually export or get my data out of these apps?
faq.a: Start with the step-by-step guide: drop your email in the form above and we'll send it. For viewing and pulling your field/yield data, our Yield Map Viewer tool [OPERATOR: confirm URL] is the companion to this comparison. Be realistic first - the AgriWebb reviews specifically flag missing bulk/NLIS download, and FBN's reviews don't mention export at all, so plan your data workflow before you commit.

faq.q: Why are these averages different from the App Store or Google Play listing?
faq.a: Because they are computed only from the reviews captured in this window - 28 for FBN and 26 for AgriWebb. Recent-window means like 2.96 and 4.15 move faster than lifetime store ratings and reflect what farmers are saying now.

faq.q: What is the source of this comparison?
faq.a: A single 2026-07-12 scrape of public store review feeds: 28 FBN reviews (spanning 2018-04-25 to 2025-11-17) and 26 AgriWebb reviews (2017-06-03 to 2026-02-23), each with its own star rating and date. We count them per star and store - no editorializing on the numbers.

distribution.query: fbn vs agriwebb / farmers business network vs agriwebb reviews
distribution.note: Answers the exact 'which rates higher and can I get my data out' question these threads ask: AgriWebb leads 4.15 to 2.96 on mean rating, and the only data-portability complaint in either dataset is one AgriWebb reviewer's NLIS-download friction - FBN's window has zero export complaints, so don't assume FBN is the more open system.
